description: TypeScript type definitions for every backend WebSocket payload. Defines the contract between the Python backend and the React frontend. Every subsequent ticket imports from this file.

types_to_define: SeedCandle: [ts: number, open: number, high: number, low: number, close: number, volume: number]

SeedPayload: { type: 'seed', symbol: string, chart_candles: SeedCandle[], company_name: string | null, fundamentals: FundamentalsData | null, metrics: { performance: PerformanceMetrics | null, volatility: VolatilityMetrics | null, volume: VolumeMetrics | null } }
TickPayload: { type: 'tick', candle: CandleDict, metrics: { performance: PerformanceMetrics, volatility: VolatilityMetrics, volume: VolumeMetrics }, ts: number }
CandleDict: { ts: number, open: number, high: number, low: number, close: number, volume: number }
FundamentalsData: { symbol, as_of_ts, company_name, market_cap, pe_ttm, eps_ttm, shares_outstanding, sector, industry }
PerformanceMetrics: { ret_1d, ret_1w, ret_1m, ret_3m, ret_6m, ret_1y } — all number | null
VolatilityMetrics: { range_intraday, vol_1w, vol_1m, vol_3m, vol_6m, vol_1y } — all number | null
VolumeMetrics: { volume_usd, vol_sma_20/50/100/200, vol_gap_20/50/100/200 } — all number | null
WsMessage: discriminated union of all payload types via 'type' field
architecture_proposal.design_notes: Use discriminated union (tagged union) on the 'type' field so TypeScript narrows automatically in switch/case. Export everything. Zero runtime cost — pure types.

responsive_strategy: N/A — Data layer. Pure TypeScript types, zero DOM involvement.

description: Design system: color palette, font stack, Tailwind CSS v4 theme tokens, and responsive breakpoint system. The aesthetic DNA of 'Bloomberg Terminal on Acid' — designed mobile-first from the root.

architecture_proposal.fonts: JetBrains Mono via Google Fonts CDN. Fallback: 'Roboto Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace. Applied globally via Tailwind v4 @theme { --font-mono: ... }.
tailwind_v4_setup: Use @theme directive (v4 native) for custom CSS variables: --color-bg, --color-neon, etc.
NO tailwind.config.js needed — Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first config
architecture_proposal.theme_ts: Export constants for Lightweight Charts colorspace (crosshair, grid, candles up/down, volume histogram). These map to the palette above.

responsive_strategy.approach: Mobile-first CSS variables with viewport-aware typography scale.
responsive_strategy.font_scaling: Base font: 11px on mobile (≤640px), 12px on tablet (641-1024px), 13px on desktop (≥1025px). Controlled via @media blocks in index.css setting --font-size-base. All components use rem/em relative to this root — one knob controls the entire density.
responsive_strategy.touch_targets: Define --touch-min: 44px for mobile tap targets (Apple HIG), --touch-min: 28px for desktop hover targets. Components use min-h-[var(--touch-min)] to guarantee accessibility on both.
responsive_strategy.safe_areas: Apply env(safe-area-inset-bottom) on the StatusBar and env(safe-area-inset-top) on the SymbolSearch header for iPhone notch/Dynamic Island respect.

description: The WebSocket Client Manager. A Zustand store that owns the WebSocket lifecycle: connect, disconnect, reconnect, symbol switching. Distributes parsed payloads to the rest of the app via Zustand slices.

store_shape.seedData: SeedPayload | null (full seed, set once per symbol)
store_shape.latestTick: TickPayload | null (most recent tick — transient, replaced every 1-3s)
store_shape.tickHistory: TickPayload[] (last 50 ticks for the Tick Stack)
store_shape.isMarketOpen: boolean (derived: true if ticks are flowing, false if only heartbeats)

actions.connect(symbol): Opens ws://host/ws/live/{symbol}. Sets status='connecting'. Attaches onopen, onmessage, onclose, onerror handlers.
actions.switchSymbol(newSymbol): Sends {action:'switch', symbol} to the existing socket. Resets seedData, tickHistory, metrics. Does NOT close/reopen the socket.
actions.handleMessage(msg): Switch on msg.type → seed: setSeedData, tick: setLatestTick + pushToTickHistory + setMetrics, company_name: setCompanyName, fundamentals: setFundamentals, heartbeat: noop (or set isMarketOpen=false), error: setConnectionError, session_expired: disconnect + reconnect.

architecture_proposal.reconnection_strategy: On unexpected close (code !== 1000): exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, max 16s). On session_expired: immediate reconnect. On idle_disconnect: reconnect only if document.visibilityState === 'visible'.
architecture_proposal.performance_critical: Use Zustand's transient update pattern for latestTick. The tick handler should NOT cause a full store re-render. Components subscribe to individual slices: useWsStore(s => s.latestTick). The tickHistory array is capped at 50 entries with unshift + slice to avoid unbounded growth.

responsive_strategy.approach: N/A — Data layer. But with two mobile-critical behavioral adaptations.
responsive_strategy.visibility_api: Listen to document.visibilityState. When the tab/app goes to background (user switches apps on iPhone), PAUSE reconnection attempts to save battery and mobile data. Resume only when visible again.
responsive_strategy.reduced_tick_history: On mobile (detected via navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0 or matchMedia), reduce tickHistory cap from 50 to 20 entries. Less RAM, fewer DOM nodes in the TickStack.

description: The main candlestick chart powered by TradingView Lightweight Charts v5. Initialized with 4500 candles from the Seed, then surgically updated bar-by-bar on each tick. Must handle symbol switches (full data replacement) without recreating the chart instance.

initialization: useRef<HTMLDivElement> for the container. useRef<IChartApi> for the chart instance.
createChart() once on mount with dark theme options from FRONT-002's theme.ts.
Subscribe to wsStore.seedData — when it changes (new symbol), call series.setData(seedCandles.map(toChartFormat)).
live_tick_updates: On each tick: candleSeries.update({ time: tick.candle.ts, open, high, low, close }). volumeSeries.update(...).
Lightweight Charts .update() is O(1) — it replaces or appends the last bar. Perfect for our 'du' semantics.
architecture_proposal.candle_format_conversion: Backend sends {ts: unixEpoch}. Lightweight Charts expects {time: unixEpoch} (UTCTimestamp). Conversion: { time: candle.ts as UTCTimestamp, open, high, low, close }. Volume series uses { time: candle.ts, value: candle.volume, color: close >= open ? greenAlpha : redAlpha }.
architecture_proposal.symbol_switch: On new seedData: series.setData(newCandles). Chart auto-adjusts scale. No destroy/recreate needed.
architecture_proposal.resize_handling: ResizeObserver on the container div → chart.resize(width, height). Cleanup on unmount.

responsive_strategy.approach: The chart is the hero on every viewport. It must NEVER have a fixed height — it fills available space.
responsive_strategy.mobile_portrait: Chart takes 100% width, height = 55vh (leaving room for MetricsGrid tabs and StatusBar below). The TickStack sidebar does NOT exist in mobile portrait — it moves to a bottom sheet.
responsive_strategy.mobile_landscape: Chart takes 100% width, height = calc(100vh - StatusBar - SymbolHeader). Full immersive chart. MetricsGrid and TickStack are hidden — accessible only via a floating toggle button (bottom-right, 40x40px).
responsive_strategy.tablet: Chart takes 65% width (TickStack takes 35%). Identical to desktop but with slightly larger touch-friendly crosshair hitbox.
responsive_strategy.desktop_4k: Chart takes 70% width. Lightweight Charts' pixel-ratio handling ensures crisp rendering on HiDPI. No blurry upscaling.
responsive_strategy.resize_observer: A single ResizeObserver on the chart container div calls chart.resize(entry.contentRect.width, entry.contentRect.height). This handles orientation changes, split-view on iPad, and browser resize seamlessly — zero manual breakpoint logic in the chart itself.
responsive_strategy.touch_interactions: Lightweight Charts has built-in touch support (pinch-to-zoom, pan). No extra work. Disable the crosshair tooltip on mobile (it covers the candles) — show price in the SymbolSearch header instead.

description: The brutalist, high-performance live order-flow style scrolling feed. Shows the last N ticks in a vertically stacked list with GPU-accelerated CSS transforms. Each row: timestamp, price, delta, volume. Color-coded green/red.

rendering: Each tick row is a <div> with transform: translateY(). New items enter at Y=0 and push existing items down.
CSS will-change: transform on the container for GPU compositing.
Each row shows: time (HH:MM:SS), close price (formatted $X,XXX.XX), price delta (close - prevClose, green/red), volume delta.
performance_optimization: The array is capped at N items. On new tick: [newTick, ...prev.slice(0, N-1)].
Use React.memo on each tick row with ts as key. Only the new row renders.
Zustand transient updates ensure only TickStack re-renders on tick, not the entire app.
CSS animation: @keyframes slideIn { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-32px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } } with duration 150ms.
architecture_proposal.styling: Monospace font. Dense rows (h-7 or 28px). Alternating bg: transparent / #111111. Price green (#00ff00) if delta > 0, red (#ff0044) if delta < 0, muted (#666666) if delta === 0.

responsive_strategy.approach: The TickStack transforms from a persistent sidebar on desktop into a collapsible bottom sheet on mobile.
responsive_strategy.desktop: Permanent right sidebar, 30% width, full height. Always visible. 50 rows, dense (h-7, 28px per row). Shows all 4 columns: time, price, delta, volume.
responsive_strategy.mobile_portrait: Completely hidden by default. Accessible via a floating 'FEED' pill button anchored bottom-right (above StatusBar). Tapping it opens a bottom sheet (CSS transform: translateY) covering the bottom 50% of the screen. The sheet shows 20 tick rows with only 3 columns (time, price, delta — volume is dropped to fit 375px width). Swipe-down or tap the pill again to dismiss. The sheet uses position:fixed + z-50 so it overlays the chart.
responsive_strategy.mobile_landscape: Hidden. Accessible via the same floating toggle. Opens as a narrow (40% width) slide-in panel from the right edge. 20 rows, 3 columns.
responsive_strategy.tablet: Persistent right sidebar (35% width). Identical to desktop layout.
responsive_strategy.row_density: On mobile, rows grow to h-9 (36px) to meet 44px touch target guidelines. On desktop, rows stay h-7 (28px) for maximum information density.
responsive_strategy.no_horizontal_scroll: Tick row layout uses flex with truncation (text-ellipsis) on the price column. The 3-column mobile layout is: time (flex-none w-16) | price (flex-1 min-w-0) | delta (flex-none w-16). Zero overflow.

description: The information-dense metrics dashboard. Three visual blocks: Performance (returns), Volatility (risk), and Volume (liquidity). Each block displays the latest computed values from the live stream. Values flash green/red on change.

architecture_proposal.data_source: useWsStore(s => s.metrics) — the { performance, volatility, volume } object from the latest tick payload.

MetricCard_component.props: { title: string, data: Record<string, number | null>, formatters: Record<string, (v: number) => string> }
MetricCard_component.rendering: Title bar (e.g., 'PERFORMANCE') + grid of key-value rows. Keys are labels like 'Ret 1D', 'Ret 1W'. Values are formatted numbers.

formatting.returns: Sign + percentage: '+2.34%' / '-1.56%'. Green if positive, red if negative.
formatting.volatility: Percentage: '18.42%'. Neutral color (muted) — vol is vol, not good/bad.
formatting.volume: Dollar format with suffixes: '$1.2B', '$345M'. Gap values as percentage with sign.

MetricCard_component.null_handling: Display '—' in muted gray for null/undefined values (insufficient data).

architecture_proposal.flash_on_change: On value change: briefly apply a bg flash (green or red pulse, 300ms CSS transition on background-color). Achieved via a useEffect comparing prev vs current values. CSS class 'flash-green' / 'flash-red' with animation-duration: 300ms.
architecture_proposal.styling: Panels: bg-[#111111], border border-[#222222], rounded-none (brutalist — no rounded corners). Title: text-xs uppercase tracking-widest text-[#666666]. Values: text-sm font-mono.

responsive_strategy.approach: Horizontal tab navigation on mobile instead of vertical stacking. Information density is preserved by showing ONE full card at a time, not three squished cards.
responsive_strategy.desktop: CSS Grid: grid-cols-3. All three MetricCards visible simultaneously in a horizontal row below the chart. Each card is a column with 6 key-value rows.
responsive_strategy.mobile_portrait: A tabbed interface. Three tab buttons at the top ('PERF' | 'VOL' | 'RISK') — only 1 card visible at a time. Active tab has a neon green bottom-border-2. Swipe left/right to switch tabs (CSS scroll-snap on a flex container with overflow-x-auto + snap-x snap-mandatory + children with snap-start min-w-full). This gives native iOS momentum scrolling with zero JavaScript. The key-value rows inside each card use a 2-column grid (3 items per row, label+value) to fit more data vertically: grid-cols-2 on mobile (2 metrics per row) vs grid-cols-1 on desktop (1 metric per row, more vertical space).
responsive_strategy.mobile_landscape: grid-cols-3 (same as desktop). Landscape gives enough width for all three panels side-by-side. Reduced vertical height per card.
responsive_strategy.no_horizontal_scroll: The scroll-snap tab container has overflow-x:auto with -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch but the INNER card content never exceeds its card width. Labels truncate with text-ellipsis. Numbers are right-aligned mono.

description: A compact horizontal bar displaying fundamental data: market cap, P/E, EPS, shares outstanding, sector, industry. Updated once on seed and on live fundamentals events.

architecture_proposal.layout: Single horizontal flex row. Each item: label (text-xs muted) above value (text-sm mono). Separator: thin vertical border between items.

formatting.sector_industry: Plain text, truncated with ellipsis if too long

architecture_proposal.null_handling: If fundamentals is null (symbol has no fundamentals, e.g., crypto), hide the entire bar. Don't show empty cards.

responsive_strategy.approach: Horizontally scrollable ticker tape on mobile. The bar NEVER wraps to multiple lines — it scrolls.
responsive_strategy.desktop: All 6 items fit in a single row (flex-row, gap-6). No scroll needed on ≥1024px.
responsive_strategy.mobile: overflow-x-auto with -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch. Items are flex-shrink-0 with fixed min-width (80px each). The user swipe-scrolls horizontally to see all fundamentals. A subtle gradient fade on the right edge (::after pseudo-element with linear-gradient from transparent to bg_primary) signals there's more content to scroll. This is the Bloomberg terminal pattern — professionals expect horizontal scroll on dense data bars.
responsive_strategy.tablet: Same as desktop (items fit in one row).
responsive_strategy.no_vertical_impact: The bar is always exactly h-10 (40px). It never grows, never wraps, never pushes the chart down unpredictably.

description: The symbol search/switch component. A command-palette style input (Ctrl+K or tap) that fetches available symbols from GET /symbols, provides autocomplete, and triggers wsStore.switchSymbol() on selection.

architecture_proposal.trigger: A compact header showing current symbol + company name. Clicking it or pressing Ctrl+K opens the search overlay.
architecture_proposal.symbol_fetching: On mount: fetch GET /symbols from the REST API. Cache in a local ref. The list is static (changes only after batch ETL runs).
architecture_proposal.autocomplete: Simple substring filter on the symbol list. No fuzzy matching needed — symbols are short uppercase strings. Show max 10 results.
architecture_proposal.selection: On Enter or tap: call wsStore.switchSymbol(selectedSymbol). Close the overlay. The Zustand store sends {action:'switch', symbol} over the existing WS.
architecture_proposal.styling: Full-screen semi-transparent overlay (#0a0a0a/80). Centered input with neon green border on focus. Results list: monospace, dense rows, highlight on hover/active.

responsive_strategy.approach: Full-screen takeover on mobile. The search is THE primary navigation — it must feel like a native app search.
responsive_strategy.desktop: Centered modal (max-w-md, 448px). Input at top, results list below. Keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter). Escape to close.
responsive_strategy.mobile: Full-screen overlay (position:fixed inset-0). Input pinned to top with auto-focus (opens the keyboard immediately). Results list fills remaining height with scroll. Each result row is h-12 (48px) for fat-finger-proof tap targets. On selection, the overlay dismisses and the keyboard closes via input.blur().
responsive_strategy.trigger_adaptation: Desktop: Ctrl+K shortcut shown as a hint badge next to the symbol. Mobile: no shortcut hint (no keyboard). Instead, the entire symbol header area is a large tap target (h-12, full-width) with a subtle search icon (magnifying glass) to signal interactivity.
responsive_strategy.keyboard_handling: On mobile, the virtual keyboard pushes the viewport. Use visualViewport API or position the input at the top (not centered) so results remain visible above the keyboard. The overlay uses flex-col: input (flex-none) at top, results (flex-1 overflow-y-auto) below.

description: A thin bottom status bar showing: connection status (dot: green=connected, yellow=reconnecting, red=disconnected), current symbol, tick count, last tick timestamp, and latency indicator.

data_sources: useWsStore(s => s.latestTick?.ts) — derive 'last update X seconds ago'
Tick count: derived from tickHistory.length or a counter in store
architecture_proposal.layout: Fixed bottom bar, bg-[#0a0a0a], border-t border-[#222222]. Flex row with items spaced evenly. Left: status dot + 'LIVE' / 'RECONNECTING' / 'OFFLINE'. Center: symbol. Right: tick count + latency.
architecture_proposal.live_clock: A useEffect with setInterval(1000) that updates 'last tick Xs ago' relative to the latest tick timestamp. Resets on each tick.

responsive_strategy.approach: Always visible on every viewport. Content density reduces on mobile — fewer labels, same critical info.
responsive_strategy.desktop: h-6 (24px). Shows: [dot LIVE] [AAPL] [Ticks: 142] [Last: 3s ago]. All items visible.
responsive_strategy.mobile: h-7 (28px, slightly taller for touch). Abbreviate: [dot] [AAPL] [142] [3s]. Drop the text labels ('Ticks:', 'Last:'), keep only the values. The dot color (green/yellow/red) provides connection status without text. Add padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for iPhone home indicator.
responsive_strategy.no_overflow: All items use flex-shrink-0 with truncation. The center symbol is the only item that can truncate (text-ellipsis) if the symbol name is absurdly long.

description: The main dashboard layout. Stitches all components into the final Bloomberg-on-Acid layout. Initializes the WebSocket connection on mount. Responsive from DNA — mobile-first CSS grid that scales UP to 4K.

architecture_proposal.initialization: useEffect on mount: wsStore.connect(defaultSymbol ?? 'BTC'). Cleanup on unmount: wsStore.disconnect().
architecture_proposal.no_loading_spinner: While seed is loading, show the empty dark layout with a single pulsing neon green dot in the chart area. The instant the seed arrives, everything populates simultaneously.

responsive_strategy.approach: CSS Grid with named template areas. ONE grid definition per breakpoint. Zero absolute positioning hacks. The grid is the single source of spatial truth.

ascii: TickStack: floating bottom-sheet (hidden by default, toggle via pill button)
mobile_portrait_layout.scroll: The entire area below the header scrolls vertically (chart + fundamentals + metrics). The chart has a fixed height (55vh) so it never collapses to nothing. overflow-y-auto on the main container.

mobile_landscape_layout.behavior: Full immersive chart mode. MetricsGrid and FundamentalsBar hide. Only the chart fills the screen. A floating toggle button (bottom-right, 40x40) opens MetricsGrid as an overlay.

tablet_layout.grid: grid-template-rows: auto auto 1fr auto auto; grid-template-cols: 65fr 35fr;

desktop_layout.grid: grid-template-rows: auto auto 1fr auto auto; grid-template-cols: 70fr 30fr;
desktop_layout.4k_note: At ≥2560px, consider increasing TickStack to 40% width and showing expanded metric labels. But V1 ships with the 70/30 split for all desktop sizes.

responsive_strategy.implementation: Use Tailwind v4 responsive prefixes: default (mobile) → sm: (≥640px tablet) → lg: (≥1024px) → xl: (≥1280px desktop). The grid is defined in Dashboard.tsx with className='grid grid-rows-[auto_55vh_auto_auto] grid-cols-1 sm:grid-rows-[auto_auto_1fr_auto_auto] sm:grid-cols-[65fr_35fr] xl:grid-cols-[70fr_30fr]'. Named grid-area classes on each child.
responsive_strategy.viewport_height: Use h-dvh (dynamic viewport height) on the root container, NOT h-screen. dvh accounts for mobile browser chrome (Safari's address bar, bottom toolbar) that steals 80-100px from 100vh. This prevents the StatusBar from being hidden behind the iOS bottom bar.
responsive_strategy.orientation_change: ResizeObserver on the root container handles all transitions. No manual window.addEventListener('resize'). The CSS grid reflows automatically — the chart's ResizeObserver fires, Lightweight Charts resizes, done.

description: Shared formatting utilities: currency ($1,234.56), percentage (+2.34%), large numbers ($1.2B), timestamps (HH:MM:SS), and delta arrows. Used by MetricsGrid, TickStack, FundamentalsBar.

functions: formatTimestamp(unixTs: number): string — 'HH:MM:SS' in local timezone
architecture_proposal.design: Pure functions, zero dependencies. Intl.NumberFormat for locale-aware number formatting. Cached formatter instances for performance.

responsive_strategy: N/A — Data layer. Pure formatting functions with zero DOM awareness. The responsive adaptation (e.g., showing '$1.2B' vs '$1,200,000,000') is already handled by formatLargeNumber — the compact format is correct for ALL viewports.
